chunks.text: The Computer Graphics Group studies scientific visualization, physically-based rendering, animation, computer-aided geometric design, rapid prototyping, computer aided cornea modeling and visualization, medical imaging, tele-immersive environments for geographically distributed collaboration, and virtual environments for surgical simulation.

Computer Aided Design and Modeling: Computer aided geometric design. Splines and subdivision. Surface optimization. Texture synthesis. Sound synthesis. Rapid prototyping. Rendering: Image based rendering. Vision realistic rendering. Computational photography and video. Visualization: Scientific and information visualization. Virtual environments, tele-immersive environments Animation: Motion capture analysis and synthesis. Physically based simulation. Video games. Art: Abstract sculpture, art & mathematics

Research Centers: Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging, Berkeley Center for New Media, Berkeley Institute of Design, Center for Augmented Cognition, Computer Graphics at Berkeley, FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality, Tele-Immersion, Video and Image Processing Lab, Visual Computing Lab

Primary Faculty: Brian A. Barsky, Alexei (Alyosha) Efros, Hany Farid, Ren Ng, James O'Brien (coordinator), Carlo H. SÃ©quin. Secondary Faculty: Ruzena Bajcsy, John F. Canny, Susan L. American Academy of Arts and Sciences Member: Ruzena Bajcsy, 2007; Susan L. Graham, 1995. Berkeley Citation: Ruzena Bajcsy, 2023; Carlo H. SÃ©quin, 2016; Susan L. Graham, 2009. Related Courses: CS 39J. Freshman/Sophomore Seminar, CS 184. Foundations of Computer Graphics, CS 274. Computational Geometry, CS C280. Computer Vision, CS 284A. Foundations of Computer Graphics
